output.market_context: Macro regime is expansionary with stable Fed funds (~3.63%), moderate GDP growth, but rising CPI and weakening consumer sentiment (49.8) — favors Tech/Discretionary/Financials, with Utilities/Staples disfavored. Tape is mid-correction in mega-cap tech (AVGO/MSFT/META/AMZN under MA20, RSI 37–44) while healthcare (UNH/XLV) and select chips (AMD/SMCI/MU) lead; insider activity is overwhelmingly selling with no cluster buys.
themes: Heavy insider selling across megacap tech (NVDA $224M, MU $38M, WMT $200M)
Defensive sectors (XLU/XLP/XLB) in bear MA stack — aligned with disfavored macro

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Healthcare leader with bullish MA stack, 20d breakout, positive news polarity and strong post-beat momentum.
ranked_candidates.narrative: UNH is the cleanest cross-signal name: bull MA stack, RSI 69.8 (close to overbought but with breakout confirmation), +8.9% 1M momentum, and high-buzz/high-polarity news flow. Earnings beat (+9.5%) still tailwind. Healthcare is technically disfavored by macro regime but XLV strength suggests rotation; no insider buys to confirm.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Top-ranked news polarity (0.98) on AI/semis rebound narrative; tech-favored macro offsets weak short-term technicals.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AVGO has the highest news composite in the universe with strong AI-enterprise positive coverage. However it sits below MA20 with RSI 42.8 after a -14% week — buy-the-dip setup but no insider confirmation. Earnings was in-line, not a tailwind. Strong macro tailwind keeps conviction above neutral.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Strong 1M momentum (+19.6%), bull MA stack, AI memory/chip narrative tailwind, and recent earnings beat.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AMD shows momentum + earnings + news alignment, with macro tilt favorable. Counter-signal: director Denzel sold ~$5.5M in early June. Technical pullback this week (-4.3%) creates an entry but insider selling caps conviction.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Massive earnings beat (+34.5%), bull MA stack, +31% 1M momentum, and high positive news polarity on AI server demand.
ranked_candidates.narrative: SMCI has multi-factor alignment: earnings, technical, news, macro all positive. Recent -5.8% weekly pullback offers entry. Low Reddit buzz (only 2 mentions) limits sentiment confirmation; no insider data either way.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: AI memory trade with +46.8% 1M momentum and positive news; offset by heavy CEO selling.
ranked_candidates.narrative: MU has the strongest 1M momentum in the universe and high positive news polarity around AI memory ('mid-innings' narrative). However CEO Mehrotra sold ~$38M across 36 transactions in late May — a serious tell that caps enthusiasm. Bull MA stack but -8.3% week suggests momentum cooling.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Healthcare sector breakout with bullish MA stack and positive news polarity, confirming the UNH thesis at sector level.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLV shows bull MA stack, RSI 62, +5.4% 1M and high positive news flow including Mizuho's 'healthcare as value' framing. Macro regime technically disfavors healthcare but flows say otherwise. Good defensive complement.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Bull MA stack, positive Buffett-narrative news flow, and earnings beat — a low-volatility ballast.
ranked_candidates.narrative: BRK.B has clean technicals (bull stack, +3.6% week) and high positive news polarity around Buffett succession/quality. No insider data (Buffett rarely sells). Solid steady-state long, modest upside.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: WWDC catalyst with strong news polarity, bull MA stack, and tech-favored macro.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AAPL has WWDC (June 8) catalyst with high news buzz/polarity, RSI 65, +8.9% 1M momentum, bull MA stack. Risk: AI narrative remains a relative weakness vs MSFT/GOOGL ('AI not existential' headlines cut both ways). No insider activity.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Bull MA stack, macro-favored financials sector with moderate news support.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLF is technically constructive (bull stack, RSI 55.7) and macro-favored. News polarity is modestly positive. Lower conviction than UNH/AVGO but a solid sector tilt.

output.summary: 3 weeks in, +2.53% cumulative vs SPY +1.74% (+93bps alpha), driven almost entirely by high-conviction semis picks (NVDA/SMCI/MU) on 5/25. Medium-conviction "diversifier" basket from cold start (XLE, BRK.B, GOOGL, AMZN) all closed at losses, and this past week gave back 62bps of alpha as the AI sleeve sold off.
what_worked: High-conviction calls dominated: 4 high-conviction picks averaged +18.9% with 75% win rate, vs medium at -2.5% / 14% win rate. MU (+37.7%), SMCI, and the NVDA add on 5/25 were the entire P&L.
Cross-signal alignment (news >90 + technical >80 + earnings beat + no insider selling) was a reliable filter — every winner had ≥3 of these green.
Avoiding insider-flagged names (WMT, AAPL, META, AMD) was correct — those names underperformed the AI sleeve materially, and the 6/1 AMZN close ahead of further weakness was timely.
TLH-driven exits on 6/1 (AMZN/GOOGL/BRK.B/XLE) recycled into MSFT/AVGO captured ~$15 in tax savings while removing dead weight.
what_missed: Cold-start 5/18 'diversification basket' was a mistake: all 4 medium-conviction openers (XLE -5.5%, BRK.B -4.1%, GOOGL -4.8%, AMZN -0.5%) closed red. News-polarity-only thesis without technical or earnings confirmation produced zero edge.
XLE was opened on news=95 alone with macro_fit=50 and no earnings catalyst — exactly the 'news strength as standalone thesis' failure mode. Lost 5.5% in 7 days.
BRK.B opened as 'ballast' lost 4% in a week — defensive ballast that drops with the tape isn't ballast; the post-Buffett tape signal was visible and ignored.
Week of 6/8 (-0.62% alpha) suggests adding MSFT/AVGO at peak AI-sleeve RSI extremes may have been late entry — chase risk on overbought-but-clean setups under-weighted.
lessons_for_pm: Medium-conviction openers have a 14% win rate so far — raise the bar. If a name doesn't clear ≥3 strong factors (news, technical, earnings, macro, insider), prefer cash or upsizing an existing high-conviction winner.
News polarity alone (factor=95, others=50) has produced 0 wins in 3 attempts (XLE, AMZN, partial GOOGL). Require at least one confirming factor (technical >70 OR earnings beat) before opening.
Continue sizing high-conviction setups meaningfully — the 5/25 NVDA add and SMCI/MU opens at half-vol-cap are the reason the book is green. Don't dilute conviction across mediocre diversifiers.
Watch entry timing on overbought-but-clean names: MSFT/AVGO opened with semis RSI 78-82 across the sleeve; consider staged entries (half now, half on pullback) when RSI >75 even if signals are clean.
'Ballast' picks (BRK.B, XLE, defensives) need their OWN bullish thesis — they are not automatic risk reducers. If technical <70 and macro_fit=50, it's not ballast, it's drag.
TLH-driven recycling worked well on 6/1; keep using realized-loss harvesting as an explicit trigger for low-conviction holdings that have drifted.
watch_for: AI/semis sleeve concentration risk: NVDA/SMCI/MU/MSFT/AVGO/QQQ — last week's -3.4% drawdown is a preview. Check correlated downside if NVDA breaks.
Entry timing into names with RSI >75 — the 6/8 weakness suggests we may be late-cycle in the AI rip; demand pullback or stage entries.
UNH remains on watch from prior reflection — small position, but if news polarity rolls negative like GOOGL did, exit don't average.

output.weekly_thesis: Harvest broken MSFT for tax credit, upsize the highest-conviction winner (UNH), and rotate into AMD + AAPL (WWDC) while trimming MU on heavy insider selling.
output.narrative: Two clear actions drive the week: (1) close MSFT to harvest a $109 loss (~$38 tax savings) since the technical thesis is broken and insider selling confirms, and (2) trim MU 50% as CEO's $38M sell-down across 36 transactions is too big to ignore. Redeploy proceeds into UNH (best cross-signal name in the book — upsize the winner per reflection lesson), a starter AMD (momentum + earnings + macro), and a small AAPL position into the WWDC catalyst. Keeping ~18% cash for optionality given the late-cycle AI rip warning and concentrated tech sleeve; BRK.B and XLE are wash-sale blocked regardless.

overrides.reason: Top news polarity 0.98, macro-favored, thesis intact; only -6% underwater and short-term setup is buy-the-dip not broken. Prefer harvesting MSFT where thesis is genuinely broken.
Loss is small, earnings beat and news polarity still elite; insider selling is concerning but not thesis-breaking. Tax savings too small to justify exit.
CEO sold $38M across 36 transactions late May — that magnitude is signal, not noise. Reflection lesson: persistent insider selling caps conviction; lock gains.
